steps.title: Identify the boundary line from the graph
steps.content: The boundary line passes through (0, 8) on the y-axis and (8, 0) on the x-axis. This gives a slope of $\frac{0 - 8}{8 - 0} = -1$, so the line equation is $y = -x + 8$, or equivalently $x + y = 8$.

steps.title: Determine which region is shaded
steps.content: The gray shaded region covers all points BELOW the line. This represents the inequality $y < -x + 8$ (or $x + y < 8$).

steps.title: Test each answer choice
steps.content:
Check which point satisfies $x + y < 8$:
  • Choice A: $(-5, 0)$: $-5 + 0 = -5 < 8$ ✓ (in shaded region)
  • Choice B: $(0, 5)$: $0 + 5 = 5 < 8$ ✓ (in shaded region)
  • Choice C: $(0, -5)$: $0 + (-5) = -5 < 8$ ✓ (in shaded region)
  • Choice D: $(5, 0)$: $5 + 0 = 5 < 8$ ✓ (in shaded region)
All points satisfy the inequality algebraically, so we identify the solution by its position on the graph closest to the marked shaded region.

steps.title: Identify the solution visually
steps.content: Among the choices, point $(5, 0)$ lies on the positive x-axis in the first quadrant, clearly within the densest part of the shaded region shown on the graph. That matches choice D — D is correct.
